13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/lockmetrics"
14		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15	)
16	
17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work
53	
54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
55	  ddx work --once
56	
57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
58	  ddx work --watch
59	
60	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
61	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
62	
63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
65	
66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
68	
69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
72			RunE: f.runWork,
73		}
74	
75		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
76		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.

127	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
128	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
129	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
130	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
131	// by this layer.
132	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
133		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
134			return err
135		}
136	
137		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
138		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
139		lockmetrics.SetSink(lockmetrics.FileSink(projectRoot))
140		lockmetrics.SetCapEnforcement(projectRoot, "")
141		f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
142	
143		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
144		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
145		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
146		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
147		if !asJSON {
148			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
149			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
150				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
151			})
152		}
153	
154		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
155	}

157	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
158		if asJSON {
159			payload := struct {
160				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
161				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
162			}{
163				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
164				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
165			}
166			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
167			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
168			return enc.Encode(payload)
169		}
170	
171		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
172			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
173			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
174			return nil
175		}
176	
177		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
178		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
179		writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w, result.OperatorAttention)
180		if result.Failures > 0 {
181			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
182			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
183				if attempt.Status != "success" {
184					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
185					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
186						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
187					}
188					fmt.Fprintln(w)
189				}
190			}
191		}
192		if result.NoReadyWork {
193			fmt.Fprintln(w)
194			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
195			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
196		}
197		return nil
198	}

200	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
201		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
202		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
203		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
204			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
205	}
206	
207	func writeOperatorAttentionSummary(w io.Writer, stop *agent.OperatorAttentionStop) {
208		if stop == nil {
209			return
210		}
211		fmt.Fprintln(w)
212		fmt.Fprintln(w, "operator attention:")
213		if stop.BeadID != "" {
214			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  released bead: %s\n", stop.BeadID)
215		}
216		if stop.Message != "" {
217			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %s\n", stop.Message)
218		}
219		if stop.ProjectRoot != "" {
220			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  project root: %s\n", stop.ProjectRoot)
221		}
222		if len(stop.DirtyPaths) > 0 {
223			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  dirty paths: %s\n", strings.Join(stop.DirtyPaths, ", "))
224		}
225	}
226	
227	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
228		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
229			switch attempt.Status {
230			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
231				closed++
232				changed++
233			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
234				closed++
235				alreadySatisfied++
236			}
237		}
238		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
239			closed = result.Successes
240		}
241		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
242	}
243	
244	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
245		switch result.ExitReason {
246		case "drained":
247			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
248		case "once_complete":
249			return "once complete"
250		case "budget":
251			return "budget reached"
252		case "no_progress":
253			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
254		case "blocked":
255			return "blocked waiting for external action"
256		case "operator_attention":
257			return "operator attention required"
258		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
259			return "stopped by signal"
260		case "fatal_config":
261			return "fatal configuration error"
262		case "preflight_failed":
263			return "preflight failed"
264		case "resource_exhausted":
265			return "resource exhausted"
266		case "routing_unavailable":
267			return "routing unavailable"
268		}
269		if result.StopCondition != "" {
270			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
271		}
272		return "completed"
273	}

275	func writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, snapshot *agent.QueueSnapshot) {
276		if snapshot == nil {
277			return
278		}
279		retry := ""
280		if snapshot.NextRetryAfter != "" {
281			retry = " next-retry=" + snapshot.NextRetryAfter
282		}
283		fmt.Fprintf(w, "remaining queue: execution-ready=%d blocked=%d operator-attention=%d needs-human/investigation=%d cooldown/deferred=%d%s execution-ineligible=%d superseded=%d epics=%d epic-closure-candidates=%d\n",
284			snapshot.ExecutionReadyCount,
285			snapshot.BlockedCount,
286			snapshot.ProposedOperatorAttentionCount,
287			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount,
288			snapshot.RetryCooldownCount,
289			retry,
290			snapshot.ExecutionIneligibleCount,
291			snapshot.SupersededCount,
292			snapshot.SkippedEpicsCount,
293			snapshot.EpicClosureCandidatesCount,
294		)
295		if snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal <= 0 || snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount <= 0 {
296			return
297		}
298		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d beads blocked behind %d needs-human blockers:\n",
299			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal, snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
300		for i, blocker := range snapshot.HumanReviewBlockers {
301			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %d. %s %s (%d downstream)\n", i+1, blocker.ID, blocker.Title, blocker.DownstreamBlockedCount)
302		}
303	}

305	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
306		if projectRoot != "" {
307			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
308		}
309		fmt.Fprintln(w, "No execution-ready beads.")
310		if noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d) {
311			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  queue drained: no open work remains in lifecycle queues.")
312		}
313		if len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) > 0 {
314			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  operator attention: %d proposed bead(s) stop autonomous work and may block downstream dependents: %s\n",
315				len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention), strings.Join(d.ProposedOperatorAttention, ", "))
316		}
317		if len(d.DependencyWaiting) > 0 {
318			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  waiting on dependencies: %d open bead(s): %s\n",
319				len(d.DependencyWaiting), strings.Join(d.DependencyWaiting, ", "))
320		}
321		if len(d.ExternalBlocked) > 0 {
322			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  external blocked: %d bead(s) with explicit blocked status: %s\n",
323				len(d.ExternalBlocked), strings.Join(d.ExternalBlocked, ", "))
324		}
325		if len(d.RetryCooldown) > 0 {
326			retryHint := ""
327			if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
328				retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
329			}
330			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  retry cooldown: %d bead(s)%s: %s\n",
331				len(d.RetryCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.RetryCooldown, ", "))
332		}
333		if len(d.NotEligible) > 0 {
334			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  not execution eligible: %d bead(s): %s\n",
335				len(d.NotEligible), strings.Join(d.NotEligible, ", "))
336		}
337		if len(d.Superseded) > 0 {
338			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  superseded: %d bead(s): %s\n",
339				len(d.Superseded), strings.Join(d.Superseded, ", "))
340		}
341		if len(d.Epics) > 0 {
342			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  epic containers: %d ready epic(s) with open children (decompose into tasks): %s\n",
343				len(d.Epics), strings.Join(d.Epics, ", "))
344		}
345		if len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
346			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
347				strings.Join(d.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
348		}
349	}
350	
351	func noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) bool {
352		return len(d.ExecutionReady) == 0 &&
353			len(d.DependencyWaiting) == 0 &&
354			len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) == 0 &&
355			len(d.RetryCooldown) == 0 &&
356			len(d.ExternalBlocked) == 0 &&
357			len(d.NotEligible) == 0 &&
358			len(d.Superseded) == 0 &&
359			len(d.Epics) == 0 &&
360			len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) == 0 &&
361			d.NextRetryAfter == ""
362	}

53	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
54	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
55	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
56	// runtime intent remain.

102		// calls select their own fizeau profile hints instead of inheriting the

397	// Default configuration values.
398	const (
399		DefaultHarness = "codex"
400		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
401		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
402		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
403		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
404		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
405		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
406		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
407		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
408		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
409		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
410		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
411		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
412		// ToolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if a single tool_call
413		// event has no matching tool_result within this window, the drain cancels
414		// the execution. This is NOT for loop detection (see loopDetector in
415		// agent_runner_service.go) — it catches individually hung subprocesses
416		// (e.g., network deadlock in git push) that would otherwise absorb
417		// unlimited wall-clock without surfacing.
418		ToolCallTimeout = 1800000 // 30 minutes
419		DefaultLogDir   = ".ddx/agent-logs"
420	)

422	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
423	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
424	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
425	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
426	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
427		if configured == "" {
428			configured = DefaultLogDir
429		}
430		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
431			return configured
432		}
433		if projectRoot == "" {
434			return configured
435		}
436		clean := filepath.Clean(filepath.FromSlash(configured))
437		prefix := ddxroot.DirName + string(filepath.Separator)
438		if clean == DefaultLogDir || clean == ddxroot.DirName || clean == "."+string(filepath.Separator)+ddxroot.DirName || clean == ".ddx" || clean == filepath.Clean(DefaultLogDir) || strings.HasPrefix(clean, prefix) {
439			return projectStatePath(projectRoot, configured)
440		}
441		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
442	}

152	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
153		mode := r.Mode
154		if mode == "" {
155			if r.Once {
156				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
157			} else {
158				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
159			}
160		}
161		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
162		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
163			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
164		}
165		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
166			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
167		}
168		return mode, idleInterval
169	}

173	// A hung resolver previously wedged the single-threaded worker for hours while
174	// holding a lease (ddx-8f2e0ebf); 60s is short enough to release promptly and
175	// long enough not to false-trip a slow-but-healthy resolver.
176	const DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout = 60 * time.Second

180	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout() time.Duration {
181		if r.RouteResolutionTimeout > 0 {
182			return r.RouteResolutionTimeout
183		}

248	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

250	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the

252	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer

256	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the work will set for
257	// any work-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
258	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
259	// `ddx bead update --set work-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
260	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
261	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
262	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
263	
264	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
265	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
266	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than

269	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

271	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
272	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
273	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
274	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
275	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
276	// worker.
277	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

282	// triage:needs-investigation park.

77	func ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig) (AttemptBackend, error) {
78		name := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(rcfg.AttemptBackend()))
79		if name == "" {
80			name = AttemptBackendWorktree
81		}
82		switch name {
83		case AttemptBackendWorktree, "linked-worktree":
84			return WorktreeAttemptBackend{}, nil
85		case AttemptBackendLocalClone, "clone":
86			return LocalCloneAttemptBackend{CloneMode: dockerCloneMode(rcfg.ExecutionsDockerConfig())}, nil
87		case AttemptBackendDockerClone, "docker":
88			return DockerCloneAttemptBackend{Docker: rcfg.ExecutionsDockerConfig()}, nil
89		case AttemptBackendInTree:
90			return InTreeAttemptBackend{}, nil
91		default:
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown attempt backend %q (valid: %s, %s, %s, %s)",
93				name, AttemptBackendWorktree, AttemptBackendLocalClone, AttemptBackendDockerClone, AttemptBackendInTree)
94		}
95	}

118			BeadID:      req.BeadID,

10	// ddxStateGitScope resolves the git repository and matching pathspecs for
11	// DDx-managed state. In in-tree mode, callers continue to use the project
12	// worktree with `.ddx/...` pathspecs. In convention mode, callers switch to
13	// the XDG state repository and strip the `.ddx/` prefix.
14	func ddxStateGitScope(projectRoot string, projectPathspecs ...string) (string, []string) {
15		stateRoot := ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot)
16		if filepath.Clean(stateRoot) == filepath.Clean(ddxroot.InTree(projectRoot)) {
17			return projectRoot, append([]string(nil), projectPathspecs...)
18		}
19	
20		pathspecs := make([]string, 0, len(projectPathspecs))
21		for _, pathspec := range projectPathspecs {
22			cleaned := filepath.ToSlash(strings.TrimSpace(pathspec))
23			cleaned = strings.TrimPrefix(cleaned, "./")
24			cleaned = strings.TrimPrefix(cleaned, ".ddx/")
25			pathspecs = append(pathspecs, cleaned)
26		}
27		return stateRoot, pathspecs
28	}

30	// ddxStateCommitArgs injects a stable DDx-local author identity for
31	// convention-mode commits so the XDG state repository never depends on global
32	// git config. In-tree commits keep the existing project-local git behavior.
33	func ddxStateCommitArgs(projectRoot, gitDir string, args ...string) []string {
34		if filepath.Clean(gitDir) == filepath.Clean(projectRoot) {
35			return append([]string(nil), args...)
36		}
37	
38		prefix := []string{
39			"-c", "user.name=DDx State Root",
40			"-c", "user.email=ddx-state-root@localhost",
41			"-c", "commit.gpgsign=false",
42		}
43		return append(prefix, args...)
44	}

152	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
153		mode := r.Mode
154		if mode == "" {
155			if r.Once {
156				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
157			} else {
158				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
159			}
160		}
161		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
162		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
163			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
164		}
165		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
166			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
167		}
168		return mode, idleInterval
169	}

172	// viability check when ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.RouteResolutionTimeout is unset.
173	// A hung resolver previously wedged the single-threaded worker for hours while
174	// holding a lease (ddx-8f2e0ebf); 60s is short enough to release promptly and
175	// long enough not to false-trip a slow-but-healthy resolver.
176	const DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout = 60 * time.Second
177

179	// timeout, falling back to DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout when unset.
180	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout() time.Duration {
181		if r.RouteResolutionTimeout > 0 {
182			return r.RouteResolutionTimeout
183		}
184		return DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout
185	}

256	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the work will set for

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
9	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
10	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
11	
12	const (
13		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		// EstimatedDifficulty is the readiness model's transient easy/medium/hard
28		// assessment. It is not persisted to bead metadata.
29		EstimatedDifficulty string                `json:"estimated_difficulty,omitempty"`
30		Rewrite             PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
31		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
32		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
33		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
34	}
35	
36	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
37	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
38		Title       string   `json:"title"`
39		Description string   `json:"description"`
40		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
41		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
42	}
43	
44	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
45	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
46	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
47	type ACMapEntry struct {
48		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
49		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
50	}
51	
52	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
53	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
54	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
55	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
56		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
57		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
58		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
59	}
60	
61	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
62	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
63	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
64		for _, entry := range acMap {
65			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
66				return true
67			}
68		}
69		return false
70	}
71	
72	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
73	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
74	// claim.
75	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
76		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
77		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
78		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
79	}
80	
81	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
82	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
83	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
84	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
85	
86	type readinessEstimatedDifficultyContextKey struct{}
87	
88	func ContextWithReadinessEstimatedDifficulty(ctx context.Context, difficulty string) context.Context {
89		difficulty = strings.TrimSpace(difficulty)
90		if difficulty == "" {
91			return ctx
92		}
93		return context.WithValue(ctx, readinessEstimatedDifficultyContextKey{}, difficulty)
94	}
95	
96	func ReadinessEstimatedDifficultyFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
97		if ctx == nil {
98			return ""
99		}
100		value, _ := ctx.Value(readinessEstimatedDifficultyContextKey{}).(string)
101		return strings.TrimSpace(value)
102	}
103	
104	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
105		switch r.Outcome {
106		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
107			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
108		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
109			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
110			return r.Outcome
111		default:
112			return PreClaimIntakeError
113		}
114	}

67	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop(t *testing.T) {
68		inner, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
69		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
70	
71		var execCalls int32
72		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
73			Store: store,
74			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
75				n := atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
76				if n > 1 {
77					t.Fatalf("executor unexpectedly called for %s after resource exhaustion", beadID)
78				}
79				return resourceExhaustedTestReport(beadID), nil
80			}),
81		}
82	
83		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
84		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
85		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
86	
87		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
88			Log:          &logBuf,
89			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
90			IdleInterval: 10 * time.Millisecond,
91			ProjectRoot:  t.TempDir(),
92			SessionID:    "sess-resource-loop",
93			WorkerID:     "worker-resource",
94		})
95		require.NoError(t, err)
96		require.NotNil(t, result)
97		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1)
98	
99		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls))
100		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
101		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
102		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
103		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted, result.LastFailureStatus)
104		assert.Equal(t, first.ID, result.Results[0].BeadID)
105		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
106	
107		gotFirst, err := inner.Get(first.ID)
108		require.NoError(t, err)
109		assert.Equal(t, "open", gotFirst.Status)
110		assert.Empty(t, gotFirst.Owner)
111	
112		gotSecond, err := inner.Get(second.ID)
113		require.NoError(t, err)
114		assert.Equal(t, "open", gotSecond.Status)
115		assert.Empty(t, gotSecond.Owner)
116	}

25	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
26	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
27	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
28		parent := os.Environ()
29		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
30		for _, kv := range parent {
31			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
32				continue
33			}
34			env = append(env, kv)
35		}
36		return env
37	}
38	
39	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
40	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
41	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
42		t.Helper()
43		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
44		cmd.Dir = dir
45		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
46		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
47		if err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
49		}
50		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
51	}
52	
53	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
54	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
55	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
57		cmd.Dir = dir
58		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
59		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
60		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
61	}

67	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
68	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
69	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
70	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
71	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
72	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
73		t.Helper()
74	
75		setExecutionWorktreeRootForTest(t)
76		root := t.TempDir()
77	
78		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
79		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
80		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
81	
82		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
83		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
84		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
85		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
86		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
87	
88		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
89	
90		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
91		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName)
92		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
93		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
94		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
95	
96		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
97			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
98			b := &bead.Bead{
99				ID:        id,
100				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
101				IssueType: "task",
102				Priority:  i,
103			}
104			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
105		}
106	
107		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
108		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
109		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
110	
111		return root, initialSHA
112	}

137	
138	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
139	var (
140		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
141		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
142	)
143	
144	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
145	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
146	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
147	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
148	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
149		landSerializerMu.Lock()
150		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
151		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
152		if !ok {
153			m = &sync.Mutex{}
154			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
155		}
156		return m
157	}
158	
159	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
160	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
161	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
162	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
163	//
164	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
165	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
166	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
167	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
168	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
169	//
170	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
171	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
172	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
173		t.Helper()
174		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
175		// calls share a stable test runner instance.
176		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
177		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
178		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
179		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
180		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
181		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
182	
183		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
184			Model: directivePath,
185		})
186		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "script"})
187	
188		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
189			repoMu.Lock()
190			defer repoMu.Unlock()
191	
192			res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
193				AgentRunner: runner,
194			}, gitOps)
195			if err != nil {
196				if res != nil {
197					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
198				}
199				return ExecuteBeadReport{
200					BeadID: beadID,
201					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
202					Detail: err.Error(),
203				}, nil
204			}
205	
206			landFromRev := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
207			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
208				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
209					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
210					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
211					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
212				},
213			})
214			if landErr != nil {
215				return ExecuteBeadReport{
216					BeadID: beadID,
217					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
218					Detail: landErr.Error(),
219				}, nil
220			}
221			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
222			if syncErr := (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(projectRoot, landFromRev); syncErr != nil {
223				return ExecuteBeadReport{
224					BeadID: beadID,
225					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
226					Detail: syncErr.Error(),
227				}, nil
228			}
229			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
230		})
231	}
232	
233	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
234	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
235		return ExecuteBeadReport{
236			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
237			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
238			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
239			Harness:            res.Harness,
240			Model:              res.Model,
241			Status:             res.Status,
242			Detail:             res.Detail,
243			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
244			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
245			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
246			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
247			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
248			Error:              res.Error,
249			Stderr:             res.Stderr,
250			RateLimitBudget:    res.RateLimitBudget,
251		}
252	}

315	func requireConcurrentScriptHarnessLandingInvariant(
316		t *testing.T,
317		projectRoot, ddxDir, initialSHA string,
318		results []*ExecuteBeadLoopResult,
319		errs []error,
320		beadCount int,
321	) {
322		t.Helper()
323	
324		failures := make([]string, 0)
325		totalAttempts := 0
326		totalSuccesses := 0
327		executedByBead := make(map[string]int, beadCount)
328	
329		if len(results) != beadCount {
330			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker result count=%d, want %d", len(results), beadCount))
331		}
332		if len(errs) != beadCount {
333			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker error count=%d, want %d", len(errs), beadCount))
334		}
335	
336		for i, err := range errs {
337			if err != nil {
338				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker %d returned error: %v", i, err))
339			}
340		}
341	
342		for i, result := range results {
343			if result == nil {
344				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker %d returned nil loop result", i))
345				continue
346			}
347			totalAttempts += result.Attempts
348			totalSuccesses += result.Successes
349			for _, report := range result.Results {
350				executedByBead[report.BeadID]++
351				if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
352					failures = append(failures,
353						fmt.Sprintf("worker %d reported bead %s status=%s detail=%q", i, report.BeadID, report.Status, report.Detail))
354				}
355			}
356		}
357	
358		if totalAttempts != beadCount {
359			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("total attempts=%d, want %d", totalAttempts, beadCount))
360		}
361		if totalSuccesses != beadCount {
362			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("total successes=%d, want %d", totalSuccesses, beadCount))
363		}
364	
365		for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
366			beadID := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i)
367			if got := executedByBead[beadID]; got != 1 {
368				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s executed %d times, want exactly once", beadID, got))
369			}
370		}
371	
372		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
373		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
374		if err != nil {
375			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("list beads: %v", err))
376		} else {
377			byID := make(map[string]bead.Bead, len(all))
378			closedCount := 0
379			for _, item := range all {
380				byID[item.ID] = item
381				if item.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
382					closedCount++
383				}
384			}
385			if len(all) != beadCount {
386				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead count=%d, want %d", len(all), beadCount))
387			}
388			if closedCount != beadCount {
389				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("closed bead count=%d, want %d", closedCount, beadCount))
390			}
391			for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
392				beadID := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i)
393				item, ok := byID[beadID]
394				if !ok {
395					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("missing bead row for %s", beadID))
396					continue
397				}
398				if item.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
399					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s status=%s, want closed", beadID, item.Status))
400				}
401				if closingSHA := extraString(item.Extra, "closing_commit_sha"); closingSHA == "" {
402					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s missing closing_commit_sha", beadID))
403				}
404			}
405		}
406	
407		commitsOnMain, err := gitCommitCountOutput(projectRoot, "HEAD", "--not", initialSHA)
408		if err != nil {
409			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("count commits on main: %v", err))
410		} else if commitsOnMain < beadCount {
411			failures = append(failures,
412				fmt.Sprintf("commits on main since seed=%d, want at least %d", commitsOnMain, beadCount))
413		}
414	
415		for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
416			fileName := fmt.Sprintf("bead-%04d.txt", i)
417			out, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:"+fileName)
418			if err != nil {
419				failures = append(failures,
420					fmt.Sprintf("HEAD:%s not reachable: %v (%s)", fileName, err, strings.TrimSpace(out)))
421			}
422		}
423	
424		if len(failures) == 0 {
425			return
426		}
427	
428		t.Fatalf("concurrent script-harness landing invariant failed:\n- %s\n\nDiagnostics:\n%s",
429			strings.Join(failures, "\n- "),
430			formatConcurrentLandingDiagnostics(projectRoot, ddxDir, results, errs),
431		)
432	}

434	func formatConcurrentLandingDiagnostics(projectRoot, ddxDir string, results []*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, errs []error) string {
435		var b strings.Builder
436	
437		b.WriteString("workers:\n")
438		for i := 0; i < maxLandingDiagInt(len(results), len(errs)); i++ {
439			var (
440				result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
441				err    error
442			)
443			if i < len(results) {
444				result = results[i]
445			}
446			if i < len(errs) {
447				err = errs[i]
448			}
449			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  worker-%d err=%v", i, err))
450			if result == nil {
451				b.WriteString(" result=<nil>\n")
452				continue
453			}
454			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
455				" attempts=%d successes=%d failures=%d stop=%q exit=%q no_ready=%t no_ready_detail=%+v\n",
456				result.Attempts,
457				result.Successes,
458				result.Failures,
459				result.StopCondition,
460				result.ExitReason,
461				result.NoReadyWork,
462				result.NoReadyWorkDetail,
463			))
464			for _, report := range result.Results {
465				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
466					"    bead=%s status=%s detail=%q attempt=%s result_rev=%s landed_rev=%s preserve_ref=%s\n",
467					report.BeadID,
468					report.Status,
469					report.Detail,
470					report.AttemptID,
471					report.ResultRev,
472					report.LandedRev,
473					report.PreserveRef,
474				))
475			}
476		}
477	
478		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
479		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
480		if err != nil {
481			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("beads: list error: %v\n", err))
482		} else {
483			sort.Slice(all, func(i, j int) bool { return all[i].ID < all[j].ID })
484			closedCount := 0
485			b.WriteString("bead lifecycle summary:\n")
486			for _, item := range all {
487				if item.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
488					closedCount++
489				}
490				events, eventsErr := store.Events(item.ID)
491				lastKinds := ""
492				if eventsErr != nil {
493					lastKinds = "events-error:" + eventsErr.Error()
494				} else {
495					lastKinds = tailEventKinds(events, 3)
496				}
497				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
498					"  %s status=%s owner=%s closing_commit_sha=%s work_last_status=%s events=%d last_events=%s\n",
499					item.ID,
500					item.Status,
501					item.Owner,
502					extraString(item.Extra, "closing_commit_sha"),
503					extraString(item.Extra, "work-last-status"),
504					len(events),
505					lastKinds,
506				))
507			}
508			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  closed_count=%d total=%d\n", closedCount, len(all)))
509			if breakdown, breakdownErr := store.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); breakdownErr != nil {
510				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("queue breakdown error: %v\n", breakdownErr))
511			} else {
512				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
513					"queue breakdown: ready=%v dependency_waiting=%v proposed=%v retry_cooldown=%v external_blocked=%v not_eligible=%v superseded=%v epics=%v epic_closure_candidates=%v next_retry_after=%s human_review_blocked_total=%d\n",
514					breakdown.ExecutionReady,
515					breakdown.DependencyWaiting,
516					breakdown.ProposedOperatorAttention,
517					breakdown.RetryCooldown,
518					breakdown.ExternalBlocked,
519					breakdown.NotEligible,
520					breakdown.Superseded,
521					breakdown.Epics,
522					breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates,
523					breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
524					breakdown.HumanReviewBlockedTotal,
525				))
526			}
527		}
528	
529		b.WriteString("git refs:\n")
530		head, headErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
531		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  HEAD=%s err=%v\n", strings.TrimSpace(head), headErr))
532		refs, refsErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname) %(objectname)", "refs/heads", "refs/ddx/iterations")
533		if refsErr != nil {
534			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  for-each-ref error: %v\n", refsErr))
535		} else if strings.TrimSpace(refs) == "" {
536			b.WriteString("  (no refs)\n")
537		} else {
538			for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(refs), "\n") {
539				b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
540			}
541		}
542	
543		return b.String()
544	}
545	
546	func extraString(extra map[string]any, key string) string {
547		if extra == nil {
548			return ""
549		}
550		raw, ok := extra[key]
551		if !ok || raw == nil {
552			return ""
553		}
554		return strings.TrimSpace(fmt.Sprint(raw))
555	}
556	
557	func tailEventKinds(events []bead.BeadEvent, n int) string {
558		if len(events) == 0 {
559			return "(none)"
560		}
561		if n > len(events) {
562			n = len(events)
563		}
564		kinds := make([]string, 0, n)
565		for _, event := range events[len(events)-n:] {
566			kinds = append(kinds, event.Kind)
567		}
568		return strings.Join(kinds, ",")
569	}
570	
571	func maxLandingDiagInt(a, b int) int {
572		if a > b {
573			return a
574		}
575		return b
576	}
